questions.question: What is 'Smishing'?
options: Phishing via Social Media
Phishing via Voice Calls
Phishing via SMS (Text Messages)
Physical theft of a hard drive

questions.question: Which protocol is used to securely browse the web by encrypting data between the browser and the server?
options: HTTP
FTP
HTTPS
SNMP

questions.question: In the context of a firewall, what does 'Stateful Inspection' mean?
options: It only checks the source IP address
It monitors the state of active connections and determines which packets to allow
It blocks all traffic from outside the country
It counts how many packets are sent per second
questions.answer: It monitors the state of active connections and determines which packets to allow

questions.question: Which of the following is a 'Zero-Day' vulnerability?
options: A bug that has existed for exactly zero days
A vulnerability that is exploited before the developer has a chance to create a patch
A virus that deletes itself after 24 hours
A password that has expired
questions.answer: A vulnerability that is exploited before the developer has a chance to create a patch

questions.question: What is the primary difference between Symmetric and Asymmetric encryption?
options: Symmetric is faster but Asymmetric uses two different keys (Public and Private)
Symmetric is only used for WiFi
Asymmetric is older and less secure
There is no difference
questions.answer: Symmetric is faster but Asymmetric uses two different keys (Public and Private)
